```{r,cache = TRUE}
#   Intercept_27 + Intercept_28 + Intercept_29 + Intercept_30 + Intercept_31 + Intercept_32 + Intercept_33 + 
#   Intercept_34 + Intercept_35 + Intercept_36 + Intercept_37 + Intercept_38 + Intercept_39 + Intercept_40 + 
#   Intercept_41 + Intercept_42 + Intercept_43 + Intercept_44 + Intercept_45 + Intercept_46 + Intercept_47 + 
#   Intercept_48 + Intercept_49 + Intercept_50 + Intercept_51 + Intercept_52 + Intercept_53 + Intercept_54 + 
#   Intercept_55 + Intercept_56 + Intercept_57 + Intercept_58 + factor(month) + 
#   aqi_resids +
#   cum_total_precip_6 +
#   avg_avg_air_temp_6 +
#   avg_avg_dew_point_6 +
#   max_avg_wind_speed_6 +
#   f(sw_binned,model = "rw2") +
#   # Spline for treatment * SDI group 
#   Group*ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_6_binned,df=3) +
#   f(ID,model = "generic0",Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar",order = 6))

kgr_formula_hierarchical3 = deaths ~ offset(log(Total_Pop/100000)) +
  f(NAME,model = "iid",hyper = list(prec = list(prior = "loggamma", param = c(10, 5))))+
  factor(month) + 
  aqi_resids +
  cum_total_precip_6 +
  avg_avg_air_temp_6 +
  avg_avg_dew_point_6 +
  max_avg_wind_speed_6 +
  f(sw_binned,model = "rw2") +
  # Spline for treatment * SDI group 
  Group*ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_6_binned,df=3) +
  f(ID,model = "generic0",Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar",order = 6))

# kgr_formula_hierarchical3 = deaths ~ -1 +
#   offset(log(Total_Pop)) +
#   factor(month) +
#   aqi_resids +
#   cum_total_precip_6 +
#   avg_avg_air_temp_6 +
#   avg_avg_dew_point_6 +
#   max_avg_wind_speed_6 +
#   sw_prod + I(sw_prod^2) + I(sw_prod^3) +
#   # Global PM2.5 dose-response curve
#   ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_6_binned,df=3) +
#   # Cluster-specific deviations from global curve (shrunken toward zero)
#   f(Z_cluster, model = spde, group = Cluster, control.group = list(model = "exchangeable")) +
#   f(ID,model = "generic0",Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar",order = 6))

tic()
model3 = inla(formula = kgr_formula_hierarchical3,family = "nbinomial",data = inla_df, num.threads = 20,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                control.fixed = prior_fixed,
                control.family = list(hyper = list(theta = list(prior = "pc.mgamma", param = 1))),
                control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))
toc()

model_summary <- model3$summary.fixed
bri_hyperpar_summary <- bri.hyperpar.summary(model3)
model_DIC <- model3$dic$dic
model_WAIC <- model3$waic$waic
preds_model <- model3$summary.fitted.values
preds_model <- cbind(inla_df$NAME, inla_df$time, inla_df$ID, inla_df$deaths, preds_model)
colnames(preds_model) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "deaths", "mean", "sd", "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")

final_model_results = list(
  model_summary = model_summary,
  bri_hyperpar_summary = bri_hyperpar_summary,
  model_DIC = model_DIC,
  model_WAIC = model_WAIC,
  fitted_values = preds_model
)

model_summary
```


### Plotting DRF from coefficients

```{r}
base_spline_summary = model_summary[80:82,]
cluster2_summary = model_summary[c(83,87,91),]
cluster3_summary = model_summary[c(84,88,92),]
cluster4_summary = model_summary[c(85,89,93),]
cluster5_summary = model_summary[c(86,90,94),]

cluster1_coefs = base_spline_summary$mean
cluster2_coefs = cluster1_coefs + cluster2_summary$mean
cluster3_coefs = cluster1_coefs + cluster3_summary$mean
cluster4_coefs = cluster1_coefs + cluster4_summary$mean
cluster5_coefs = cluster1_coefs + cluster5_summary$mean

spline_basis = ns(unique(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_6_binned), df = 3)

cluster1_drf <- as.numeric(spline_basis %*% cluster1_coefs)
cluster2_drf <- as.numeric(spline_basis %*% cluster2_coefs)
cluster3_drf <- as.numeric(spline_basis %*% cluster3_coefs)
cluster4_drf <- as.numeric(spline_basis %*% cluster4_coefs)
cluster5_drf <- as.numeric(spline_basis %*% cluster5_coefs)

# For uncertainty bands (simplified approach)
# Extract standard errors
cluster1_se = base_spline_summary$sd
cluster2_se = sqrt(cluster1_se^2 + cluster2_summary$sd^2)
cluster3_se = sqrt(cluster1_se^2 + cluster3_summary$sd^2)
cluster4_se = sqrt(cluster1_se^2 + cluster4_summary$sd^2)
cluster5_se = sqrt(cluster1_se^2 + cluster5_summary$sd^2)

# Calculate uncertainty bands
cluster1_drf_se <- sqrt(rowSums((spline_basis %*% diag(cluster1_se^2)) * spline_basis))
cluster2_drf_se <- sqrt(rowSums((spline_basis %*% diag(cluster2_se^2)) * spline_basis))
cluster3_drf_se <- sqrt(rowSums((spline_basis %*% diag(cluster3_se^2)) * spline_basis))
cluster4_drf_se <- sqrt(rowSums((spline_basis %*% diag(cluster4_se^2)) * spline_basis))
cluster5_drf_se <- sqrt(rowSums((spline_basis %*% diag(cluster5_se^2)) * spline_basis))

# Combine into data frame
z_values = unique(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_6_binned)

cluster_curves <- rbind(
  data.frame(Z = z_values, DRF = cluster1_drf, 
             lower = cluster1_drf - 1.96 * cluster1_drf_se,
             upper = cluster1_drf + 1.96 * cluster1_drf_se,
             Cluster = "1"),
  data.frame(Z = z_values, DRF = cluster2_drf,
             lower = cluster2_drf - 1.96 * cluster2_drf_se,
             upper = cluster2_drf + 1.96 * cluster2_drf_se,
             Cluster = "2"),
  data.frame(Z = z_values, DRF = cluster3_drf,
             lower = cluster3_drf - 1.96 * cluster3_drf_se,
             upper = cluster3_drf + 1.96 * cluster3_drf_se,
             Cluster = "3"),
  data.frame(Z = z_values, DRF = cluster4_drf,
             lower = cluster4_drf - 1.96 * cluster4_drf_se,
             upper = cluster4_drf + 1.96 * cluster4_drf_se,
             Cluster = "4"),
  data.frame(Z = z_values, DRF = cluster5_drf,
             lower = cluster5_drf - 1.96 * cluster5_drf_se,
             upper = cluster5_drf + 1.96 * cluster5_drf_se,
             Cluster = "5")
)

# Define colors
my_colors <- c("darkblue", "purple", "magenta", "orange", "yellow")

# Plot
ggplot(cluster_curves, aes(x = Z, y = DRF, color = Cluster, fill = Cluster)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = my_colors) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = my_colors) +
  labs(x = "Cumulative PM2.5 (binned)", 
       y = "DRF Effect",
       title = "Cluster-Specific Dose-Response Functions",
       subtitle = "With 95% Confidence Bands") +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Posterior predictive check for zero inflation

```{r,cache = TRUE}
zip_formula = deaths ~ -1 + offset(log(Total_Pop)) + 
  factor(month) + 
  aqi_resids +
  cum_total_precip_6 +
  avg_avg_air_temp_6 +
  avg_avg_dew_point_6 +
  max_avg_wind_speed_6 +  
  f(sw_binned,model = "rw2") +
  group*ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_6_binned,df=3) +
  f(ID,model = "generic0",Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar",order = 6))


###Try zeroinflatedpoisson0, zeroinflatedpoisson1, zeroinflatednbinomial0, zeroinflatednbinomial1
zip_model = inla(formula = zip_formula,family = "zeroinflatedpoisson0",data = inla_df, num.threads = 1,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                #control.family = list(hyper = list(theta = list(prior = "pc.mgamma", param = 1))),
                control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))

preds_zip_model <- zip_model$summary.fitted.values
preds_zip_model <- cbind(inla_df$NAME, inla_df$time, inla_df$ID, inla_df$deaths, preds_zip_model)
colnames(preds_zip_model) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "deaths", "mean", "sd", "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")
```

```{r}
kgr_formula_hierarchical3 = deaths ~ -1 + offset(log(Total_Pop)) + 
  factor(month) + 
  aqi_resids +
  cum_total_precip_6 +
  avg_avg_air_temp_6 +
  avg_avg_dew_point_6 +
  max_avg_wind_speed_6 +
  f(sw_binned,model = "rw2") +
  group*ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_6_binned,df=3) +
  f(ID,model = "generic0",Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar",order = 6))

model3 = inla(formula = kgr_formula_hierarchical3,family = "poisson",data = inla_df, num.threads = 20,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                #control.family = list(hyper = list(theta = list(prior = "pc.mgamma", param = 1))),
                control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))
```

```{r}
###ZIP
###Posterior predictive sampling
p_density = data.frame(zip_model$marginals.hyperpar$`zero-probability parameter for zero-inflated poisson_0`)
sample_p = inla.rmarginal(100000,inla.smarginal(p_density)) 
pp_psamples = sample_p  

pp_zip_samples = list()

###Generate uncertainty bands from the posterior predictive distribution as opposed to posterior of the linear predictor
for (i in 1:nrow(preds_zip_model)){ ###RENDER ERROR HERE
  ###Sample 1000 mu for each row
  row = data.frame(zip_model$marginals.fitted.values[[i]])

  sample_lambda = inla.rmarginal(100000,inla.smarginal(row))
  lower = quantile(sample_lambda,0.025)
  mean = mean(sample_lambda)
  upper = quantile(sample_lambda,0.975)

  sample_y = ifelse(rbinom(100000, size = 1, prob = sample_p) > 0, 0, rpois(100000, lambda = sample_lambda))
  pp_zip_samples[[i]] = sample_y
}

###Poisson
preds_pois_model <- model3$summary.fitted.values
preds_pois_model <- cbind(inla_df$NAME, inla_df$time, inla_df$ID, inla_df$deaths, preds_pois_model)
colnames(preds_pois_model) <- c("county", "time", "ID", "deaths", "mean", "sd", "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")

###Posterior predictive sampling
pp_pois_samples = list()

###Generate uncertainty bands from the posterior predictive distribution as opposed to posterior of the linear predictor
for (i in 1:nrow(preds_pois_model)){
    ###Sample 1000 mu for each row
    row = data.frame(model3$marginals.fitted.values[[i]])
  
    sample_lambda = inla.rmarginal(100000,inla.smarginal(row))
    lower = quantile(sample_lambda,0.025)
    mean = mean(sample_lambda)
    upper = quantile(sample_lambda,0.975)
  
    sample_y = rpois(n=100000,lambda = sample_lambda)
    pp_pois_samples[[i]] = sample_y
  }

###PPC
true_prop0 = length(which(inla_df$deaths == 0)) / nrow(inla_df)
true_mv = mean(inla_df$deaths) / var(inla_df$deaths)

# ###ZIP
pp_zip_sample_df = do.call(rbind,pp_zip_samples)

zip_prop0 = c()
zip_mv = c()

for(i in 1:1000){
  deaths = pp_zip_sample_df[,i]
  zip_prop0[i] = length(which(deaths == 0)) / length(deaths)
  zip_mv[i] = mean(deaths) / var(deaths)
}


# ###POISSON
pp_pois_sample_df = do.call(rbind,pp_pois_samples)

pois_prop0 = c()
pois_mv = c()

for(i in 1:1000){
  deaths = pp_pois_sample_df[,i]
  pois_prop0[i] = length(which(deaths == 0)) / length(deaths)
  pois_mv[i] = mean(deaths) / var(deaths)
}


hist(zip_prop0,xlim = c(0.1,0.3),main = "ZIP Prop 0")
abline(v = true_prop0)

hist(pois_prop0,xlim = c(0.1,0.3),main = "Poisson Prop 0")
abline(v = true_prop0)


hist(zip_mv,xlim = c(0.006,0.008),main = "ZIP Mean/Var")
abline(v = true_mv)

hist(pois_mv,xlim = c(0.006,0.008),main = "Poisson Mean/Var")
abline(v = true_mv)


###Split into groups to parse signal
small_counties = na.omit(counties[which(CApops$POPESTIMATE2019 <= 100000)])
small_idx = which(inla_df$NAME %in% small_counties)
large_idx = which(!(inla_df$NAME %in% small_counties))

inla_df_small = inla_df[small_idx,]
inla_df_large = inla_df[large_idx,]

###Small
small_prop0 = length(which(inla_df_small$deaths == 0)) / nrow(inla_df_small)
small_mv = mean(inla_df_small$deaths) / var(inla_df_small$deaths)

zip_small_prop0 = c()
zip_small_mv = c()

for(i in 1:100000){
  deaths = pp_zip_sample_df[small_idx,i]
  zip_small_prop0[i] = length(which(deaths == 0)) / length(deaths)
  zip_small_mv[i] = mean(deaths) / var(deaths) 
}


pois_small_prop0 = c()
pois_small_mv = c()

for(i in 1:100000){
  deaths = pp_pois_sample_df[small_idx,i]
  pois_small_prop0[i] = length(which(deaths == 0)) / length(deaths)
  pois_small_mv[i] = mean(deaths) / var(deaths) 
}

hist(zip_small_prop0,xlim = c(0.1,0.3),main = "ZIP Prop 0 (Small Counties)")
abline(v = small_prop0)

hist(pois_small_prop0,xlim = c(0.1,0.3),main = "Poisson Prop 0 (Small Counties)")
abline(v = small_prop0)


hist(zip_small_mv,xlim = c(0.004,0.006),main = "ZIP Mean/Var (Small Counties)")
abline(v = small_mv)

hist(pois_small_mv,xlim = c(0.004,0.006),main = "Poisson Mean/Var (Small Counties)")
abline(v = small_mv)

###Large
large_prop0 = length(which(inla_df_large$deaths == 0)) / nrow(inla_df_large)
large_mv = mean(inla_df_large$deaths) / var(inla_df_large$deaths)

zip_large_prop0 = c()
zip_large_mv = c()

for(i in 1:100000){
  deaths = pp_zip_sample_df[large_idx,i]
  zip_large_prop0[i] = length(which(deaths == 0)) / length(deaths)
  zip_large_mv[i] = mean(deaths) / var(deaths) 
}


pois_large_prop0 = c()
pois_large_mv = c()

for(i in 1:100000){
  deaths = pp_pois_sample_df[large_idx,i]
  pois_large_prop0[i] = length(which(deaths == 0)) / length(deaths)
  pois_large_mv[i] = mean(deaths) / var(deaths) 
}

hist(zip_large_prop0,xlim = c(0.1,0.3),main = "ZIP Prop 0 (Large Counties)")
abline(v = large_prop0)

hist(pois_large_prop0,xlim = c(0.1,0.3),main = "Poisson Prop 0 (Large Counties)")
abline(v = large_prop0)


hist(zip_large_mv,xlim = c(0.02,0.025),main = "ZIP Mean/Var (Large Counties)")
abline(v = large_mv)

hist(pois_large_mv,xlim = c(0.02,0.025),main = "Poisson Mean/Var (Large Counties)")
abline(v = large_mv)
```

## Sensitivity analysis for unmeasured confounding

```{r,cache = TRUE}
#Create negative control proxies (Z = future exposure, W = past outcome)
inla_df <- inla_df %>%
  group_by(ID) %>%
  mutate(
    W1 = lag(deaths, 1),
    W4 = lag(deaths, 3),
    W8 = lag(deaths, 6),
    W12 = lag(deaths, 12),
    Zp1 = lead(cum_monthly_total_pm25_6,1),
    Zp4 = lead(cum_monthly_total_pm25_6,3),
    Zp8 = lead(cum_monthly_total_pm25_6,6),
    Zp12 = lead(cum_monthly_total_pm25_6,12)
  ) %>% ungroup()

#Fit model with proxies to see if nonexistent associations appear 
kgr_formula_hierarchical_wproxy = W1 ~ -1 + Intercept_1 + Intercept_2 + Intercept_3 + Intercept_4 + Intercept_5 + 
  Intercept_6 + Intercept_7 + Intercept_8 + Intercept_9 + Intercept_10 + Intercept_11 + Intercept_12 + 
  Intercept_13 + Intercept_14 + Intercept_15 + Intercept_16 + Intercept_17 + Intercept_18 + Intercept_19 + 
  Intercept_20 + Intercept_21 + Intercept_22 + Intercept_23 + Intercept_24 + Intercept_25 + Intercept_26 + 
  Intercept_27 + Intercept_28 + Intercept_29 + Intercept_30 + Intercept_31 + Intercept_32 + Intercept_33 + 
  Intercept_34 + Intercept_35 + Intercept_36 + Intercept_37 + Intercept_38 + Intercept_39 + Intercept_40 + 
  Intercept_41 + Intercept_42 + Intercept_43 + Intercept_44 + Intercept_45 + Intercept_46 + Intercept_47 + 
  Intercept_48 + Intercept_49 + Intercept_50 + Intercept_51 + Intercept_52 + Intercept_53 + Intercept_54 + 
  Intercept_55 + Intercept_56 + Intercept_57 + Intercept_58 + factor(month) + 
  aqi_resids +
  cum_total_precip_6 +
  avg_avg_air_temp_6 +
  avg_avg_dew_point_6 +
  max_avg_wind_speed_6 +
  f(sw_binned,model = "rw2") +
  # Global PM2.5 dose-response curve
  ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_6_binned,df=3) + Zp1 +
  # Cluster-specific deviations from global curve (shrunken toward zero)
  f(Z_cluster, model = spde, group = Cluster, control.group = list(model = "exchangeable")) +
  f(ID,model = "generic0",Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar",order = 6))

proxy_model = inla(formula = kgr_formula_hierarchical_wproxy,family = "nbinomial",data = inla_df, num.threads = 5,
                control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
                control.family = list(hyper = list(theta = list(prior = "pc.mgamma", param = 1))),
                control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
                control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))

proxy_model$summary.fixed
proxy_model$summary.hyperpar
```

### FOR REFERENCE: 

```{r}
detach("package:MASS", unload = TRUE) 
plot((CA_sf %>% mutate(clus = clus5_min$groups))['clus'], main = "5 cluster example with population constraint")
```

```{r}
inla_df %>% group_by(NAME) %>% summarize(avg_z = mean(cum_monthly_total_pm25_6), max_z = max(cum_monthly_total_pm25_6), avg_y = mean(deaths), max_y = max(deaths), avg_yrate = mean(death_rate), max_yrate = max(death_rate)) %>% arrange(max_z)
```

# What is DRF? DRF models the expected value of potential outcomes Y as a function of the treatment level and covariates

## Example with z = 1000

```{r,eval = FALSE}
#Attach duplicate dataset with specified treatment/dose level 
# duplicate_df = inla_df
duplicate_df = inla_df %>% filter(NAME %in% counties[46:58])
# duplicate_df = inla_df %>% filter(NAME == "Kings")

duplicate_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_6_binned = 1000
duplicate_df$deaths = NA 

inla_cf_df = rbind(inla_df,duplicate_df)
cf_idx = c((nrow(inla_df)+1):nrow(inla_cf_df))

prior_fixed = list(mean = list(default = 0), 
                   prec = list(default = 0.001,`group2:1` = 5,
                               `group3:1` = 5,`group4:1` = 5,
                               `group5:1` = 5,`group2:2` = 5,
                               `group3:2` = 5,`group4:2` = 5,
                               `group5:2` = 5,`group2:3` = 5,
                               `group3:3` = 5,`group4:3` = 5,
                               `group5:3` = 5))

# Same formula as above
kgr_formula = deaths ~ -1 + Intercept_1 + Intercept_2 + Intercept_3 + Intercept_4 + Intercept_5 + 
        Intercept_6 + Intercept_7 + Intercept_8 + Intercept_9 + Intercept_10 + Intercept_11 + Intercept_12 + 
        Intercept_13 + Intercept_14 + Intercept_15 + Intercept_16 + Intercept_17 + Intercept_18 + Intercept_19 + 
        Intercept_20 + Intercept_21 + Intercept_22 + Intercept_23 + Intercept_24 + Intercept_25 + Intercept_26 + 
        Intercept_27 + Intercept_28 + Intercept_29 + Intercept_30 + Intercept_31 + Intercept_32 + Intercept_33 + 
        Intercept_34 + Intercept_35 + Intercept_36 + Intercept_37 + Intercept_38 + Intercept_39 + Intercept_40 + 
        Intercept_41 + Intercept_42 + Intercept_43 + Intercept_44 + Intercept_45 + Intercept_46 + Intercept_47 + 
        Intercept_48 + Intercept_49 + Intercept_50 + Intercept_51 + Intercept_52 + Intercept_53 + Intercept_54 + 
        Intercept_55 + Intercept_56 + Intercept_57 + Intercept_58 + factor(month) + 
        aqi_resids +
        cum_total_precip_6 +
        avg_avg_air_temp_6 +
        avg_avg_dew_point_6 +
        max_avg_wind_speed_6 +
        f(sw_binned,model = "rw2") +
        # Spline for treatment * SDI group 
        group*ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_6_binned,df=3) +
        f(ID,model = "generic0",Cmatrix = solve(H^2), group = time, control.group = list(model = "ar",order = 6))


model = inla(formula = kgr_formula, family = "nbinomial", data = inla_cf_df, num.threads = 1,
             control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
             control.fixed = prior_fixed,
             control.family = list(hyper = list(theta = list(prior = "pc.mgamma", param = 1))),
             control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
             control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))



###Extract relevant information and store in the list
model_summary <- model$summary.fixed
bri_hyperpar_summary <- bri.hyperpar.summary(model)
model_DIC <- model$dic$dic
model_WAIC <- model$waic$waic

preds_model <- model$summary.fitted.values
preds_model <- cbind(inla_cf_df$NAME, inla_cf_df$time, inla_cf_df$date, 
                     inla_cf_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_6_binned,
                     inla_cf_df$deaths, preds_model)
colnames(preds_model) <- c("county", "time", "date", "exposure", "deaths", "mean", 
                           "sd", "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")

a = preds_model[cf_idx,]
mean(a$mean)

# a = preds_model[cf_idx,] %>% filter(county == "Kings")
# mean(a$mean)
```

## Function to evaluate DRFs for grid of treatment values (ran as background job with tmux)

```{r}
dose_grid = c(0,10,20,50,100,200,500,1000,2000,3000)

bins = list(c(1:15),c(16:30),c(31:45),c(46:58))

DRF_curves = function(dataset,dose_grid){
  cf_preds_list = list()
  
  for(i in 1:length(dose_grid)){
    cat("Calculating DRF for dose level:", dose_grid[i], "\n")
    
    # Initialize combined predictions dataframe for this dose
    combined_cf_preds = NULL
    
    for (j in 1:4){
      cat("  Processing batch", j, "of 4\n")
  
      # Get counties for this batch
      batch_counties = counties[bins[[j]]]
      
      # Create duplicate dataset for just these counties
      duplicate_df = dataset[dataset$NAME %in% batch_counties,]
      duplicate_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_6_binned = dose_grid[i]
      duplicate_df$deaths = NA
      
      # Combine original dataset with counterfactual data for this batch
      inla_cf_df = rbind(dataset, duplicate_df)
      cf_idx = c((nrow(dataset)+1):nrow(inla_cf_df))

      kgr_formula_hierarchical = deaths ~ -1 +
        offset(log(Total_Pop)) +
        factor(month) +
        aqi_resids +
        cum_total_precip_6 +
        avg_avg_air_temp_6 +
        avg_avg_dew_point_6 +
        max_avg_wind_speed_6 +
        # Global PM2.5 dose-response curve
        ns(cum_monthly_total_pm25_6_binned,df = 3) +
        # Cluster-specific deviations from global curve (shrunken toward zero)
        f(Z_cluster, model = spde, group = Cluster, control.group = list(model = "exchangeable")) +
        f(id2, model = "generic0", Cmatrix = inv_covGP2_response)
      
      # Error handling for inla()
      kgr_model <- tryCatch({
        inla(formula = kgr_formula_hierarchical, family = "nbinomial", data = inla_cf_df, num.threads = 50,
             control.compute = list(dic=TRUE,waic=TRUE,return.marginals.predictor=TRUE),
             #control.fixed = prior_fixed,
             control.family = list(hyper = list(theta = list(prior = "pc.mgamma", param = 1))),
             control.inla = list(strategy = "simplified.laplace"),
             control.predictor = list(compute = TRUE, link = 1))
      }, error = function(e) {
        cat("Error fitting INLA model at dose", dose_grid[i], "batch", j, ":", conditionMessage(e), "\n")
        return(NULL)
      })
      
      if (is.null(kgr_model)) {
        cat("Skipping batch", j, "due to model fitting error\n")
        next
      }
      
      # Extract predictions for counterfactual data
      preds_model <- kgr_model$summary.fitted.values
      preds_model <- cbind(inla_cf_df$NAME, inla_cf_df$time, inla_cf_df$date, 
                           inla_cf_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_6_binned, inla_cf_df$deaths, preds_model)
      colnames(preds_model) <- c("county", "time", "date", "exposure", "deaths", "mean", 
                                 "sd", "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")
      
      # Get predictions for this batch's counterfactual data
      batch_cf_preds = preds_model[cf_idx,]
      
      # Append to combined predictions
      if (is.null(combined_cf_preds)) {
        combined_cf_preds = batch_cf_preds
      } else {
        combined_cf_preds = rbind(combined_cf_preds, batch_cf_preds)
      }
    }
    
    # Store combined predictions for this dose level
    cf_preds_list[[i]] = combined_cf_preds
  }
  
  return(cf_preds_list)
}

# full_data_DRFs = DRF_curves(inla_df,dose_grid)
# wildfire_szn_DRFs = DRF_curves(wildfire_szn_df,dose_grid)
```

## Bootstrapped version prototype

see estimate-drfs-v4.R or parallel-test.R

# ADRF: average of DRF over all individuals in the population

## Function to calculate ADRFs for grid of treatment values 

```{r}
# dose_grid = c(0,10,20,50,100,200,500,1000,2000,3000)
dose_grid = c(0,10,20,50,100,200,400,600,800,1000)

DRF_to_ADRF = function(DRF_list,dataset){
  ADRF = data.frame(Z = dose_grid, mu_hat = NA, weighted_mu_hat = NA, rate_hat = NA, weighted_rate_hat = NA)
  county_pops = dataset %>% select(NAME,date,Total_Pop) 

  for (i in 1:length(DRF_list)){
    dose_results = DRF_list[[i]]
    dose_results_pop = dose_results %>% left_join(county_pops,by = c("county" = "NAME","date" = "date"))
    
    # Calculate population-weighted mean
    total_pop = sum(dose_results_pop$Total_Pop)
    pop_weights = dose_results_pop$Total_Pop / total_pop
    
    ADRF$mu_hat[i] = mean(dose_results_pop$mean)
    #ADRF$weighted_mu_hat[i] = sum(dose_results_pop$mean * pop_weights)
    #ADRF$rate_hat[i] = mean((dose_results_pop$mean / dose_results_pop$Total_Pop * 100000)) # avg county level rate
    ADRF$rate_hat[i] = sum(dose_results_pop$mean)/total_pop*100000 # avg individual level rate
    #ADRF$weighted_rate_hat[i] = sum((dose_results_pop$mean / dose_results_pop$Total_Pop * 100000) * pop_weights)
  }

  return(ADRF)
}

# #Multiplicative spatiotemporal random effect: ar6 for time, graph filter for space + nonlinear SW + group*treatment interaction
# full_data_DRFs = readRDS("full_data_drfs_NB_totalcum6_swprodrw2_ar6xgfilter_groupint_9.30.rds") #strong shrinkage

collect_bootstrap_results = function(bootstrap_results){
  DRF_list = list()
  
  for (i in 1:length(dose_grid)){
    dose_results = bootstrap_results[[1]][[i]]
    
    for (j in 2:length(bootstrap_results)){
      dose_results = rbind(dose_results,bootstrap_results[[j]][[i]])
    }
    
    
    ###FILTER OUT BAD BOOTSTRAP SAMPLES (samples or just predictions???)
    bad_preds = which(dose_results$mean >= 10000)

    if(length(bad_preds) > 0){
      bad_sims = unique(dose_results$sample_id[bad_preds])
      good_idx = which(!(dose_results$sample_id %in% bad_sims))
      dose_results = dose_results[good_idx,]
    }
    
    DRF_list[[i]] = dose_results
  }
  
  return(DRF_list)
}

###stabilized_IPW_kgr estimated on treatment NOT cum_treatment
# full_data_DRFs = readRDS("full_data_bootstrapped_drfs_totalcum6_v2_kgr2_6.23.rds") #based on 5 samples
# wildfire_szn_DRFs = readRDS("wildfire_szn_bootstrapped_drfs_totalcum6_v2_kgr2_6.23.rds") #based on 5 sample

full_data_DRFs = readRDS("full_data_parallel_bootstrapped_drfs_totalcum6_kgr2_8.13.rds") #vague prior, 100 samples
#full_data_DRFs = readRDS("full_data_parallel_bootstrapped_drfs_totalcum6_kgr2_7.26.rds") #strong shrinkage, 100 samples
#full_data_DRFs = readRDS("full_data_parallel_bootstrapped_drfs_totalcum6_kgr2_7.27.rds") #no shrinkage, 100 samples

bootstrapped_DRFs = collect_bootstrap_results(full_data_DRFs)


DRF_to_ADRF2 = function(DRF_list, dataset) {
  ADRF = data.frame(
    Z = dose_grid, 
    # Unweighted estimates
    mu_hat = NA, mu_hat_lower = NA, mu_hat_upper = NA, 
    rate_hat = NA, rate_hat_lower = NA, rate_hat_upper = NA
    # Weighted estimates
    # weighted_mu_hat = NA, weighted_mu_hat_lower = NA, weighted_mu_hat_upper = NA,
    # weighted_rate_hat = NA, weighted_rate_hat_lower = NA, weighted_rate_hat_upper = NA
  )
  
  county_pops = dataset %>% select(NAME, date, Total_Pop) 
  
  for (i in 1:length(DRF_list)) {
    
    dose_results = DRF_list[[i]]
    dose_results_pop = dose_results %>% 
      left_join(county_pops, by = c("county" = "NAME", "date" = "date"))
    
    # Calculate both weighted and unweighted estimates for each sample
    dose_summary = dose_results_pop %>% 
      group_by(sample_id) %>% 
      summarize(
        # Unweighted estimates
        sample_mu_hat = mean(mean),
        sample_rate_hat = sum(mean)/sum(Total_Pop)*100000,
        # sample_rate_hat = mean(mean / Total_Pop * 100000),

                
        # # Weighted estimates  
        # sample_weighted_mu_hat = sum(mean * Total_Pop) / sum(Total_Pop),
        # sample_weighted_rate_hat = sum((mean / Total_Pop * 100000) * Total_Pop) / sum(Total_Pop),
        
        .groups = "drop"
      )
    
    # Calculate credible intervals for unweighted estimates
    ADRF$mu_hat[i] = mean(dose_summary$sample_mu_hat)
    ADRF$mu_hat_lower[i] = quantile(dose_summary$sample_mu_hat, 0.025)
    ADRF$mu_hat_upper[i] = quantile(dose_summary$sample_mu_hat, 0.975)
    
    ADRF$rate_hat[i] = mean(dose_summary$sample_rate_hat)
    ADRF$rate_hat_lower[i] = quantile(dose_summary$sample_rate_hat, 0.025)
    ADRF$rate_hat_upper[i] = quantile(dose_summary$sample_rate_hat, 0.975)
    
    # # Calculate credible intervals for weighted estimates
    # ADRF$weighted_mu_hat[i] = mean(dose_summary$sample_weighted_mu_hat)
    # ADRF$weighted_mu_hat_lower[i] = quantile(dose_summary$sample_weighted_mu_hat, 0.025)
    # ADRF$weighted_mu_hat_upper[i] = quantile(dose_summary$sample_weighted_mu_hat, 0.975)
    # 
    # ADRF$weighted_rate_hat[i] = mean(dose_summary$sample_weighted_rate_hat)
    # ADRF$weighted_rate_hat_lower[i] = quantile(dose_summary$sample_weighted_rate_hat, 0.025)
    # ADRF$weighted_rate_hat_upper[i] = quantile(dose_summary$sample_weighted_rate_hat, 0.975)
  }
  
  return(ADRF)
}

#full_data_ADRF = DRF_to_ADRF2(bootstrapped_DRFs,inla_df)
#wildfire_szn_ADRF = DRF_to_ADRF2(wildfire_szn_DRFs,wildfire_szn_df)
```

### NEWEST ITERATION

```{r}
# for (i in 1:length(bootstrap_sample_1)){

#                                         "mean", "sd", "0.025quant", "0.5quant", "0.975quant", "mode")

#             bootstrap_sample_5,bootstrap_sample_6,bootstrap_sample_7,bootstrap_sample_8,
#             bootstrap_sample_9,bootstrap_sample_10)

parallel_bootstrap_test_results = readRDS("~/work/cum6-bootstrap-samples/parallel_bootstrap_test_results-10.9.rds")

bootstrapped_DRFs = collect_bootstrap_results(parallel_bootstrap_test_results)
full_data_ADRF = DRF_to_ADRF2(bootstrapped_DRFs,inla_df)
```  


## Plot ADRF curves

```{r}
ggplot(data = full_data_ADRF, aes(x = Z)) + 
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = rate_hat_lower, ymax = rate_hat_upper), alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_line(aes(y = rate_hat), color = "red", size = 1.2) + 
  labs(x = "cum_monthly_total_pm25_6", y = "rate_hat", title = "Overall ADRF") + 
  theme_classic()

# ggplot(data = wildfire_szn_ADRF, aes(x = Z)) + 
#   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = rate_hat_lower, ymax = rate_hat_upper), alpha = 0.3) +
#   geom_line(aes(y = rate_hat), color = "red", size = 1.2) + 
#   labs(x = "cum_monthly_total_pm25_6", y = "rate_hat", title = "Wildfire Season ADRF") + 
#   theme_classic()
```

## Calculate ADRF all counties -> make a plot: 

```{r}
county_DRF_to_ADRF = function(DRF_list, dataset) {
  county_ADRF_list = list()
  
  # Get county populations (single value per county)
  county_pops = dataset %>% select(NAME,date,Total_Pop) 
  
  for (i in counties) {
    ADRF = data.frame(Z = dose_grid, mu_hat = NA, weighted_mu_hat = NA, 
                      rate_hat = NA, weighted_rate_hat = NA)
    
    for (j in 1:length(DRF_list)) {
      county_preds = DRF_list[[j]] %>% filter(county == i)
      
      # Skip if no predictions for this county
      if (nrow(county_preds) == 0) next
      
      # Merge with time-specific populations
      county_preds_pop = county_preds %>%
        left_join(county_pops, by = c("county" = "NAME", "date" = "date"))
      
      # Population-weighted mean across time periods
      total_pop = sum(county_preds_pop$Total_Pop)
      pop_weights = county_preds_pop$Total_Pop / total_pop
      
      ADRF$mu_hat[j] = mean(county_preds_pop$mean)
      #ADRF$weighted_mu_hat[j] = sum(county_preds_pop$mean * pop_weights)
      #ADRF$rate_hat[j] = mean((county_preds_pop$mean / county_preds_pop$Total_Pop * 100000)) 
      ADRF$rate_hat[j] = sum(county_preds_pop$mean)/total_pop*100000 
      #ADRF$weighted_rate_hat[j] = sum((county_preds_pop$mean / county_preds_pop$Total_Pop * 100000) * pop_weights)
    }
    
    county_ADRF_list[[i]] = ADRF
  }
  
  return(county_ADRF_list)
}

plot_ADRFs = function(overall_ADRF, county_ADRFs, response,
                      hi.res = FALSE,input_title = "Estimated ADRFs",
                      prefix = "cum6-",filename = "county-adrfs.png") {
  
  county_data_combined <- bind_rows(county_ADRFs, .id = "county_id")
  
  if (hi.res) {
    county_data_combined <- county_data_combined %>% filter(Z <= 200)
    overall_ADRF <- overall_ADRF %>% filter(Z <= 200)
  }
  
  # Main ADRF plot
  p_main <- ggplot() + 
    geom_line(data = county_data_combined, 
              aes(x = Z, 
                  y = .data[[response]], 
                  group = county_id),
              color = "gray70", 
              alpha = 0.3, 
              size = 0.5) +
    geom_line(data = overall_ADRF, 
              aes(x = Z, y = .data[[response]]),
              color = "red", 
              size = 1.2) +
    labs(x = "cum_monthly_total_pm25_6", 
         y = response, 
         title = input_title) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 10)
    )
  
  # Marginal histogram/density
  p_top <- ggplot(inla_df, aes(x = cum_monthly_total_pm25_6)) +
    geom_density(fill = "gray80", alpha = 1.5) +  
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      plot.margin = margin(0, 5, 0, 5) # reduce gap
    )
  
  # Combine: p_top above p_main
  combined <- p_top / p_main + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 3))
  
#   ggsave(
#   filename = paste0(prefix,filename),
#   plot = combined,
#   path = "~/work/important-plots",
#   width = 8,
#   height = 10,
#   dpi = 600
# )
  
  return(combined)
}
```


```{r}
county_ADRFs = county_DRF_to_ADRF(bootstrapped_DRFs,inla_df)
plot_ADRFs(full_data_ADRF,county_ADRFs,response = "rate_hat",hi.res = FALSE)
plot_ADRFs(full_data_ADRF,county_ADRFs,response = "rate_hat",hi.res = TRUE,prefix = "cum6-hi.res-")
```

## Same analysis by SDI SKATER clusters and SDI groups/clusters

```{r}
cluster_DRF_to_ADRF = function(DRF_list, dataset, clusterlabels) {
  cluster_ADRF_list = list()
  
  # Get county populations and merge with cluster labels
  county_pops = dataset %>% 
    select(NAME, date, Total_Pop) %>%
    left_join(clusterlabels, by = c("NAME" = "counties"))

  for (cluster_id in 1:num_clus) {
    ADRF = data.frame(Z = dose_grid, mu_hat = NA, weighted_mu_hat = NA, rate_hat = NA, weighted_rate_hat = NA)
    
    # Get counties in this cluster
    cluster_counties = clusterlabels$counties[clusterlabels$Cluster == cluster_id]
    
    for (j in 1:length(DRF_list)) {
      # Get predictions for all counties in this cluster
      cluster_preds = DRF_list[[j]] %>% 
        filter(county %in% cluster_counties)
      
      # Skip if no predictions for this cluster
      if (nrow(cluster_preds) == 0) next
      
      # Merge with time-specific populations and cluster info
      cluster_preds_pop = cluster_preds %>%
        left_join(county_pops, by = c("county" = "NAME", "date" = "date"))
      
      # Population-weighted mean across time periods and counties in cluster
      total_pop = sum(cluster_preds_pop$Total_Pop, na.rm = TRUE)
      pop_weights = cluster_preds_pop$Total_Pop / total_pop
      
      ADRF$mu_hat[j] = mean(cluster_preds_pop$mean, na.rm = TRUE)
      #ADRF$weighted_mu_hat[j] = sum(cluster_preds_pop$mean * pop_weights, na.rm = TRUE)
      ADRF$rate_hat[j] = mean((cluster_preds_pop$mean / cluster_preds_pop$Total_Pop * 100000), na.rm = TRUE) 
      #ADRF$rate_hat[j] = sum(cluster_preds_pop$mean)/total_pop*100000 ??? not exactly the same
      #ADRF$weighted_rate_hat[j] = sum((cluster_preds_pop$mean / cluster_preds_pop$Total_Pop * 100000) * pop_weights, na.rm = TRUE)
    }
    
    cluster_ADRF_list[[as.character(cluster_id)]] = ADRF
  }
  
  return(cluster_ADRF_list)
}

SDIgroup_DRF_to_ADRF = function(DRF_list, dataset, group_labels) {
  cluster_ADRF_list = list()
  
  # Get county populations and merge with cluster labels
  county_pops = dataset %>% 
    select(NAME, date, Total_Pop) %>%
    left_join(group_labels, by = c("NAME" = "counties"))

  for (cluster_id in 1:num_clus) {
    ADRF = data.frame(Z = dose_grid, mu_hat = NA, weighted_mu_hat = NA, rate_hat = NA, weighted_rate_hat = NA)
    
    # Get counties in this cluster
    cluster_counties = group_labels$counties[group_labels$Group == cluster_id]
    
    for (j in 1:length(DRF_list)) {
      # Get predictions for all counties in this cluster
      cluster_preds = DRF_list[[j]] %>% 
        filter(county %in% cluster_counties)
      
      # Skip if no predictions for this cluster
      if (nrow(cluster_preds) == 0) next
      
      # Merge with time-specific populations and cluster info
      cluster_preds_pop = cluster_preds %>%
        left_join(county_pops, by = c("county" = "NAME", "date" = "date"))
      
      # Population-weighted mean across time periods and counties in cluster
      total_pop = sum(cluster_preds_pop$Total_Pop, na.rm = TRUE)
      pop_weights = cluster_preds_pop$Total_Pop / total_pop
      
      ADRF$mu_hat[j] = mean(cluster_preds_pop$mean, na.rm = TRUE)
      #ADRF$weighted_mu_hat[j] = sum(cluster_preds_pop$mean * pop_weights, na.rm = TRUE)
      #ADRF$rate_hat[j] = mean((cluster_preds_pop$mean / cluster_preds_pop$Total_Pop * 100000), na.rm = TRUE)
      ADRF$rate_hat[j] = sum(cluster_preds_pop$mean)/total_pop*100000
      #ADRF$weighted_rate_hat[j] = sum((cluster_preds_pop$mean / cluster_preds_pop$Total_Pop * 100000) * pop_weights, na.rm = TRUE)
    }
    
    cluster_ADRF_list[[as.character(cluster_id)]] = ADRF
  }
  
  return(cluster_ADRF_list)
}

plot_cluster_ADRFs = function(overall_ADRF, cluster_ADRFs, response, 
                              hi.res = FALSE, input_title = "Estimated Cluster ADRFs",
                              prefix = "cum6-",filename = "cluster-adrfs.png") {
  # Convert list of dataframes to single dataframe with cluster identifier
  cluster_data_combined <- bind_rows(cluster_ADRFs, .id = "cluster_id")
  
  if(hi.res == TRUE){
    cluster_data_combined = cluster_data_combined %>% filter(Z <= 200)
    overall_ADRF = overall_ADRF %>% filter(Z <= 200)
  }
  
  # Create a color palette for clusters
  cluster_colors <- c("darkblue", "purple", "magenta", "orange", "yellow")
  names(cluster_colors) = unique(cluster_data_combined$cluster_id)
  
  # Create the base plot with cluster curves
  p_main <- ggplot() + 
    # Cluster curves first
    geom_line(data = cluster_data_combined, 
              aes(x = Z, 
                  y = cluster_data_combined[,response], 
                  group = cluster_id,
                  color = cluster_id),
              alpha = 0.7, 
              size = 0.8) +
    # Then overall curve
    geom_line(data = overall_ADRF, 
              aes(x = Z, 
                  y = overall_ADRF[,response]),
              color = "black", 
              size = 1.2) +
    scale_color_manual(values = cluster_colors, name = "Cluster") +
    labs(x = "cum_monthly_total_pm25_6", 
         y = response, 
         title = input_title) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
      legend.position = "right"
    )
  
  # Marginal histogram/density
  p_top <- ggplot(inla_df, aes(x = cum_monthly_total_pm25_6)) +
    geom_density(fill = "gray80", alpha = 1.5) +  
    theme_void() +
    theme(
      plot.margin = margin(0, 5, 0, 5) # reduce gap
    )

  # Combine: p_top above p_main
  combined <- p_top / p_main + plot_layout(heights = c(1, 3))
  
  # ggsave(
  #   filename = paste0(prefix,filename),
  #   plot = combined,
  #   path = "~/work/important-plots",
  #   width = 8,
  #   height = 10,
  #   dpi = 600
  # )
  
  return(combined)
}

# Example usage:
#cluster_ADRFs = cluster_DRF_to_ADRF(bootstrapped_DRFs, inla_df, clusterlabels)
cluster_ADRFs = SDIgroup_DRF_to_ADRF(bootstrapped_DRFs, inla_df, group_labels)

plot_cluster_ADRFs(full_data_ADRF, cluster_ADRFs, hi.res = FALSE,"rate_hat")
plot_cluster_ADRFs(full_data_ADRF, cluster_ADRFs, hi.res = TRUE,"rate_hat",prefix = "cum6-hi.res-")
```

# AGTE: evaluate ADRF at two levels of treatment for which we want to compare and take the difference

## Function to calculate 

```{r}
estimate_AGTE = function(z1,z0,ADRF_curve){
  # AGTE1 = (ADRF_curve$mu_hat[ADRF_curve$Z == z1]) - (ADRF_curve$mu_hat[ADRF_curve$Z == z0])
  # AGTE2 = (ADRF_curve$rate_hat[ADRF_curve$Z == z1]) - (ADRF_curve$rate_hat[ADRF_curve$Z == z0])
  AGTE3 = (ADRF_curve$rate_hat[ADRF_curve$Z == z1]) / (ADRF_curve$rate_hat[ADRF_curve$Z == z0])

  return(AGTE3)
}


#MAX VS MIN CONTRAST
estimate_AGTE(1000,0,full_data_ADRF)
# wildfire_szn_tau_hat = estimate_AGTE(3000,0,wildfire_szn_ADRF)

#2 SD CONTRAST
mean(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_6)
sd(inla_df$cum_monthly_total_pm25_6)
tau_hat = estimate_AGTE(1000,200,full_data_ADRF)

#Should we interpolate curve?
# f = splinefun(full_data_ADRF$Z,full_data_ADRF$weighted_rate_hat,method = "natural")
# 
# x = c(185,530,875)
# ls(envir = environment(f))
# splinecoef <- get("z", envir = environment(f))
# curve(f(x), 0, 1000, col = "green", lwd = 1.5)
# points(splinecoef, col = "purple",pch = 20, cex = 2)
# 
# new_z = c(185,530,875)
# interpolated_y = f(new_z)
# interpolated_y[3] / interpolated_y[1]


#THOMAS FIRE CASE STUDY (12/17-1/18)
thomas_fire_smoke = inla_df %>% filter(NAME %in% c("Santa Barbara","Ventura"),
  date >= as.Date("2017-11-01") & date <= as.Date("2018-02-01")) %>%
  select(NAME,date,monthly_total_pm25,cum_monthly_total_pm25_6,deaths)

###SB went from 0-541 in that month, Ventura went from 0-424
estimate_AGTE(600,0,full_data_ADRF)

tau_hat
# wildfire_szn_tau_hat
```

## Function to calculate with uncertainty quantification

```{r}
estimate_AGTE2 = function(DRF_list, dataset, z1, z0, type = "ratio") {
  # type: "difference" for Y(z1) - Y(z0), "ratio" for Y(z1) / Y(z0)
  
  # Find indices for the specified treatment levels
  z1_idx = which(dose_grid == z1)
  z0_idx = which(dose_grid == z0)
  
  if (length(z1_idx) == 0 | length(z0_idx) == 0) {
    stop("Specified treatment levels not found in dose_grid")
  }
  
  # Get the DRF results for both treatment levels
  z1_results = DRF_list[[z1_idx]]
  z0_results = DRF_list[[z0_idx]]
  
  county_pops = dataset %>% select(NAME, date, Total_Pop)
  
  # Join population data
  z1_results_pop = z1_results %>% 
    left_join(county_pops, by = c("county" = "NAME", "date" = "date"))
  z0_results_pop = z0_results %>% 
    left_join(county_pops, by = c("county" = "NAME", "date" = "date"))
  
  # Calculate estimates for each bootstrap sample
  z1_summary = z1_results_pop %>% 
    group_by(sample_id) %>% 
    summarize(
      # Unweighted estimates
      sample_mu_hat = mean(mean),
      sample_rate_hat = sum(mean)/sum(Total_Pop)*100000,
      
      # # Weighted estimates  
      # sample_weighted_mu_hat = sum(mean * Total_Pop) / sum(Total_Pop),
      # sample_weighted_rate_hat = sum((mean / Total_Pop * 100000) * Total_Pop) / sum(Total_Pop),
      
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  z0_summary = z0_results_pop %>% 
    group_by(sample_id) %>% 
    summarize(
      # Unweighted estimates
      sample_mu_hat = mean(mean),
      sample_rate_hat = sum(mean)/sum(Total_Pop)*100000,
      
      # # Weighted estimates  
      # sample_weighted_mu_hat = sum(mean * Total_Pop) / sum(Total_Pop),
      # sample_weighted_rate_hat = sum((mean / Total_Pop * 100000) * Total_Pop) / sum(Total_Pop),
      
      .groups = "drop"
    )
  
  # Merge by sample_id to ensure proper pairing
  agte_data = z1_summary %>% 
    inner_join(z0_summary, by = "sample_id", suffix = c("_z1", "_z0"))
  
  # Calculate AGTE for each bootstrap sample
  if (type == "difference") {
    agte_data = agte_data %>% 
      mutate(
        # Unweighted AGTE
        agte_mu = sample_mu_hat_z1 - sample_mu_hat_z0,
        agte_rate = sample_rate_hat_z1 - sample_rate_hat_z0
        
        # # Weighted AGTE
        # agte_weighted_mu = sample_weighted_mu_hat_z1 - sample_weighted_mu_hat_z0,
        # agte_weighted_rate = sample_weighted_rate_hat_z1 - sample_weighted_rate_hat_z0
      )
  } else if (type == "ratio") {
    agte_data = agte_data %>% 
      mutate(
        # Unweighted AGTE
        agte_mu = sample_mu_hat_z1 / sample_mu_hat_z0,
        agte_rate = sample_rate_hat_z1 / sample_rate_hat_z0
        
        # # Weighted AGTE
        # agte_weighted_mu = sample_weighted_mu_hat_z1 / sample_weighted_mu_hat_z0,
        # agte_weighted_rate = sample_weighted_rate_hat_z1 / sample_weighted_rate_hat_z0
      )
  } else {
    stop("type must be either 'difference' or 'ratio'")
  }
  
  # Calculate point estimates and confidence intervals
  agte_results = data.frame(
    z1 = z1,
    z0 = z0,
    type = type,
    
    # Unweighted estimates
    agte_mu_hat = mean(agte_data$agte_mu),
    agte_mu_lower = quantile(agte_data$agte_mu, 0.025),
    agte_mu_upper = quantile(agte_data$agte_mu, 0.975),
    
    agte_rate_hat = mean(agte_data$agte_rate),
    agte_rate_lower = quantile(agte_data$agte_rate, 0.025),
    agte_rate_upper = quantile(agte_data$agte_rate, 0.975)
    
    # # Weighted estimates
    # agte_weighted_mu_hat = mean(agte_data$agte_weighted_mu),
    # agte_weighted_mu_lower = quantile(agte_data$agte_weighted_mu, 0.025),
    # agte_weighted_mu_upper = quantile(agte_data$agte_weighted_mu, 0.975),
    # 
    # agte_weighted_rate_hat = mean(agte_data$agte_weighted_rate),
    # agte_weighted_rate_lower = quantile(agte_data$agte_weighted_rate, 0.025),
    # agte_weighted_rate_upper = quantile(agte_data$agte_weighted_rate, 0.975)
  )
  rownames(agte_results) = NULL
  
  return(agte_results)
}

estimate_AGTE2(bootstrapped_DRFs,inla_df,1000,0,"ratio")
estimate_AGTE2(bootstrapped_DRFs,inla_df,1000,0,"ratio")[7:9]
tau_hat = as.numeric(estimate_AGTE2(bootstrapped_DRFs,inla_df,1000,0,"ratio")[7])
```

3## Interpret + visualize 

```{r}
#LA 
inla_df %>% filter(NAME == "Los Angeles") %>% dplyr::select(Total_Pop,deaths,death_rate,cum_monthly_total_pm25_6) %>% summary()

4.2*10059182/100000 #Mean death rate -> deaths
(4.2*tau_hat)*10059182/100000 #Elevated death rate -> deaths

#Alameda
inla_df %>% filter(NAME == "Alameda") %>% dplyr::select(Total_Pop,deaths,death_rate,cum_monthly_total_pm25_6) %>% summary()

3.3*1613263/100000 #Mean death rate -> deaths
(3.3*tau_hat)*1613263/100000 #Elevated death rate -> deaths

#Alpine
inla_df %>% filter(NAME == "Alpine") %>% dplyr::select(Total_Pop,deaths,death_rate,cum_monthly_total_pm25_6) %>% summary()

0.08*33539/100000 #Mean death rate -> deaths
(0.08*tau_hat)*33539/100000 #Elevated death rate -> deaths

#SB
inla_df %>% filter(NAME == "Santa Barbara") %>% dplyr::select(Total_Pop,deaths,death_rate,cum_monthly_total_pm25_6) %>% summary()

3.123*439727/100000 #Mean death rate -> deaths
(3.123*tau_hat)*439727/100000 #Elevated death rate -> deaths
```

```{r}
visualize_causal_effect = function(ADRF_curve,z0 = 0,contrast = "ratio"){
  if(contrast == "difference"){
    causal_diff = data.frame(Z = dose_grid,diff = NA)
    
    for (i in 1:nrow(ADRF_curve)){
      ref_idx = which(ADRF_curve$Z == z0)
      causal_diff$diff[i] = ADRF_curve$rate_hat[i] - ADRF_curve$rate_hat[ref_idx]
    }
    
    p <- ggplot() + 
      geom_line(data = causal_diff, 
                aes(x = Z, y = diff),
                color = "red", 
                size = 1.2) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
      labs(x = "cum_monthly_total_pm25_6", 
           y = "Difference", 
           title = "Causal Difference Compared to Z = 0") +
      theme_classic() +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10)
      )
    
    print(p)
    
  } else{
    risk_ratio = data.frame(Z = dose_grid,RR = NA)
    
    for (i in 1:nrow(ADRF_curve)){
      ref_idx = which(ADRF_curve$Z == z0)
      risk_ratio$RR[i] = ADRF_curve$rate_hat[i] / ADRF_curve$rate_hat[ref_idx]
    }
    
    p <- ggplot() + 
      geom_line(data = risk_ratio, 
                aes(x = Z, y = RR),
                color = "red", 
                size = 1.2) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
      labs(x = "cum_monthly_total_pm25_6", 
           y = "Risk Ratio", 
           title = "Causal Risk Ratio Compared to Z = 0") +
      theme_classic() +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10)
      )
    
    print(p)
  }
}

visualize_causal_effect(full_data_ADRF)
# visualize_causal_effect(county_ADRFs[[50]])
# visualize_causal_effect(cluster_ADRFs[[5]])
```

```{r}
visualize_causal_effect_with_ci = function(ADRF_curve, z0 = 0, contrast = "ratio"){
  
  if(contrast == "difference"){
    causal_diff = data.frame(
      Z = ADRF_curve$Z,
      diff = NA,
      diff_lower = NA,
      diff_upper = NA
    )
    
    ref_idx = which(ADRF_curve$Z == z0)
    
    for (i in 1:nrow(ADRF_curve)){
      
      result = estimate_AGTE2(bootstrapped_DRFs,inla_df,
                              dose_grid[i],dose_grid[ref_idx],"difference")
      
      causal_diff$diff[i] = result$agte_mu_hat
      causal_diff$diff_lower[i] = result$agte_mu_lower
      causal_diff$diff_upper[i] = result$agte_mu_upper
    }
    
    p <- ggplot(causal_diff, aes(x = Z, y = diff)) + 
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = diff_lower, ymax = diff_upper),
                  fill = "red", alpha = 0.2) +
      geom_line(color = "red", size = 1.2) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
      labs(x = "cum_monthly_total_pm25_6", 
           y = "Difference", 
           title = paste0("Causal Difference Compared to Z = ", z0)) +
      theme_classic() +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10)
      )
    
    print(p)
    
  } else {
    risk_ratio = data.frame(
      Z = ADRF_curve$Z,
      RR = NA,
      RR_lower = NA,
      RR_upper = NA
    )
    
    ref_idx = which(ADRF_curve$Z == z0)
    
    for (i in 1:nrow(ADRF_curve)){
      
      result = estimate_AGTE2(bootstrapped_DRFs,inla_df,
                              dose_grid[i],dose_grid[ref_idx],"ratio")
      
      risk_ratio$RR[i] = result$agte_rate_hat
      risk_ratio$RR_lower[i] = result$agte_rate_lower
      risk_ratio$RR_upper[i] = result$agte_rate_upper
    }
    
    p <- ggplot(risk_ratio, aes(x = Z, y = RR)) + 
      geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = RR_lower, ymax = RR_upper),
                  fill = "red", alpha = 0.2) +
      geom_line(color = "red", size = 1.2) +
      geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
      labs(x = "cum_monthly_total_pm25_6", 
           y = "Risk Ratio", 
           title = paste0("Causal Risk Ratio Compared to Z = ", z0)) +
      theme_classic() +
      theme(
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 12),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 11),
        axis.text = element_text(size = 10)
      )
    
    print(p)
  }
}

visualize_causal_effect_with_ci(full_data_ADRF)
# visualize_causal_effect_with_ci(county_ADRFs[[50]])
# visualize_causal_effect_with_ci(cluster_ADRFs[[5]])


###SOMETHING WRONG HERE
for (i in 1:5){
  visualize_causal_effect_with_ci(cluster_ADRFs[[i]])
}
```


### Interpret for each county (applies AGTE estimate to each county)

```{r}
interpret_AGTE_by_county = function(AGTE_estimate,dataset){
  
  AGTE_by_county = data.frame(county = counties,avg_pop = NA,avg_death_rate = NA,new_death_rate = NA,
                              avg_deaths = NA,new_deaths = NA)
  
  for(i in 1:length(counties)){
    county_data = dataset %>% filter(NAME == counties[i]) %>% dplyr::select(Total_Pop,death_rate)
    AGTE_by_county$avg_pop[i] = mean(county_data$Total_Pop)
    
    AGTE_by_county$avg_death_rate[i] = mean(county_data$death_rate)
    AGTE_by_county$new_death_rate[i] = mean(county_data$death_rate) * AGTE_estimate
    
    AGTE_by_county$avg_deaths[i] = mean(county_data$death_rate)*mean(county_data$Total_Pop)/100000
    AGTE_by_county$new_deaths[i] = (mean(county_data$death_rate) * AGTE_estimate)*mean(county_data$Total_Pop)/100000
  }
  
  return(AGTE_by_county)
}

AGTE_by_county = interpret_AGTE_by_county(AGTE_estimate = tau_hat, dataset = inla_df)
```

## Visualize effect on death rate

### Grouped barplot

```{r}
#Visualize
# Reshape data for grouped barplot
plot_data <- AGTE_by_county %>%
  dplyr::select(county, avg_death_rate, new_death_rate) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(avg_death_rate, new_death_rate),
               names_to = "rate_type",
               values_to = "death_rate") %>%
  mutate(
    rate_type = case_when(
      rate_type == "avg_death_rate" ~ "Average Rate",
      rate_type == "new_death_rate" ~ "New Rate"
    )
  )

# Create grouped barplot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = county, y = death_rate, fill = rate_type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Average Rate" = "steelblue", "New Rate" = "coral"),
                    name = "Rate Type") +
  labs(title = "Death Rate Comparison by County",
       x = "County",
       y = "Death Rate (per 100k)") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )
```

### Stacked barplot

```{r}
#Visualize
# Calculate the increase and reshape for stacked barplot
plot_data <- AGTE_by_county %>%
  mutate(increase = new_death_rate - avg_death_rate) %>%
  dplyr::select(county, avg_death_rate, increase) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(avg_death_rate, increase),
               names_to = "component",
               values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    component = case_when(
      component == "avg_death_rate" ~ "Base Rate",
      component == "increase" ~ "Increase"
    ),
    component = factor(component, levels = c("Increase","Base Rate"))
  )

# Create stacked barplot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = county, y = value, fill = component)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Base Rate" = "steelblue", "Increase" = "coral"),
                    name = "Component") +
  labs(title = "Death Rate: Base vs Increase by County",
       x = "County",
       y = "Death Rate (per 100k)") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )
```

## Visualize effect on death counts

### Grouped barplot

```{r}
#Visualize
# Reshape data for grouped barplot
plot_data <- AGTE_by_county %>%
  dplyr::select(county, avg_deaths, new_deaths) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(avg_deaths, new_deaths),
               names_to = "count_type",
               values_to = "deaths") %>%
  mutate(
    count_type = case_when(
      count_type == "avg_deaths" ~ "Average Deaths",
      count_type == "new_deaths" ~ "New Deaths"
    )
  )

# Create grouped barplot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = county, y = deaths, fill = count_type)) +
  geom_col(position = "dodge", width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Average Deaths" = "steelblue", "New Deaths" = "coral"),
                    name = "County Type") +
  labs(title = "Death Counts Comparison by County",
       x = "County",
       y = "Respiratory Related Deaths") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )
```

### Stacked barplot

```{r}
#Visualize
# Calculate the increase and reshape for stacked barplot
plot_data <- AGTE_by_county %>%
  mutate(increase = new_deaths - avg_deaths) %>%
  dplyr::select(county, avg_deaths, increase) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(avg_deaths, increase),
               names_to = "component",
               values_to = "value") %>%
  mutate(
    component = case_when(
      component == "avg_deaths" ~ "Baseline",
      component == "increase" ~ "Increase"
    ),
    component = factor(component, levels = c("Increase","Baseline"))
  )

# Create stacked barplot
ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = county, y = value, fill = component)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Baseline" = "steelblue", "Increase" = "coral"),
                    name = "Component") +
  labs(title = "Deaths: Base vs Increase by County",
       x = "County",
       y = "Respiratory Related Deaths") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )
```

## Visualize relative risk for each county 

```{r}
county_pct_change = data.frame(county = counties, delta = NA)

for (i in 1:length(county_ADRFs)){
  county_pct_change$delta[i] = estimate_AGTE(1000,0,county_ADRFs[[i]])
}

county_pct_change %>% ggplot(aes(x=county, y=delta)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +   labs(title = "Relative Risk in Death Rate by County",
       x = "County",
       y = "Relative Risk in Death Rate") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )
```


## Visualize relative risk for each cluster 

```{r}
cluster_pct_change = data.frame(Group = c("1","2","3","4","5"), delta = NA)

for (i in 1:length(cluster_ADRFs)){
  cluster_pct_change$delta[i] = estimate_AGTE(1000,0,cluster_ADRFs[[i]])
}

my_colors <- c("darkblue", "purple", "magenta", "orange", "yellow")

cluster_pct_change %>% ggplot(aes(x=Group, y=delta, fill=Group)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") + scale_fill_manual(values = my_colors) +
  labs(title = "Relative Risk in Death Rate by Cluster",
       x = "Cluster",
       y = "Relative Risk in Death Rate") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 10),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )
```
